evals.name: reimbursable-field
evals.prompt: I sometimes pay for things that friends or the office pay me back for later. I want to be able to mark an expense as reimbursable, and later mark it as settled once I'm paid back. Old entries should just behave as normal non-reimbursable expenses. Build this into the app's data layer (schema + any supporting logic + tests) — leave the UI for a later PR.
evals.expected_output: A version(4) migration appended (v1-v3 blocks untouched), optional field(s) on Expense handled everywhere, upgrade-path + fresh-install tests, backup import tolerating old files, all gates green.
expectations: Appends version(4): existing version(1–3) blocks are byte-for-byte untouched in the diff
Upgrade-path test exists: builds a pre-change db, reopens via createDb(name), asserts old rows behave as plain non-reimbursable expenses
New Expense field(s) are optional; old rows lacking them are handled (no non-optional type, no crash on undefined)
Backups without the new field still import (parser tolerates absence) and the new field round-trips export→import; tests cover it
All three gates pass in the final logs: npm test, npm run lint, npm run build

evals.name: builtin-travel-category
evals.prompt: Add 'Travel' as a built-in category with the ✈️ emoji. It needs to appear for existing users of the app after they update, not just on fresh installs, and it should behave exactly like the other built-in categories (undeletable, merges cleanly when importing a backup from another install).
evals.expected_output: Travel added to DEFAULT_CATEGORIES with a stable cat-* id AND delivered to existing installs via a new appended version block; both paths produce identical category sets; label-clash edge handled; gates green.
expectations: Existing installs gain Travel through a newly appended version block with an upgrade(), not merely an edit to DEFAULT_CATEGORIES
Fresh installs seed Travel too, and fresh-install and upgraded databases end with identical category sets (seed test updated to 9)
Travel uses a stable cat-* id with the epoch-stepped createdAt convention so picker order and cross-install backup merging stay stable
Upgrade handles a database where the user already created a 'Travel' category: no duplicate label, no crashed upgrade; a test covers this
The delete guard treats Travel as built-in (cannot be deleted)
All three gates pass: npm test, npm run lint, npm run build

evals.name: currency-index
evals.prompt: Filtering history by currency does a full scan today. Add an IndexedDB index on the expense's currency and a listExpensesByCurrency(currency) function in the data layer (with tests) so we can query by currency efficiently.
evals.expected_output: A new version block adding the currency index (existing blocks untouched), an upgrade-path test proving data survives, and a tested listExpensesByCurrency using where('currency') with newest-first sort; gates green.
expectations: Appends a new version block adding the currency index; existing version blocks untouched
Upgrade-path test: a pre-change db with data reopens through createDb and the data is intact and queryable via the new index
listExpensesByCurrency queries via where('currency') (uses the index) and sorts newest-first like the other list functions; tested
All three gates pass: npm test, npm run lint, npm run build
